programmes.show_description: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show. Rob Beckett, Ed Gamble and Donna and Verona go up against Katherine Ryan, Dr Ranj Singh and Sara Barron.
Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock and Jon Richardson battle it out in an extended edition of the show with guests Olly Murs, Kimberly Wyatt, Matthew Crosby and Russell Peters.
An extended edition of the show with host Jimmy Carr, captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, and guests Jack Dee, Stacey Solomon, David O'Doherty and Alex James.
Manny's First Day: A drunk Bernard offers Manny a job at the bookshop then tries to get rid of him by putting him on a one-day trial.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Gary Findley, CEO of Restoration 1, a self-described 'Redneck CEO' from Texas, goes undercover to ensure that there are no flaws in the company's playbook.
Paul Damico, the former president and CEO of Round Table Pizza, goes undercover to learn ways to expand the West Coast pizza franchise eastward and attract a younger clientele.
In East Anglia, the dinner party competition features a performance from some unlikely rappers, a confusing spaghetti marrow main course, too many cooks, and a fire alarm.
In Newcastle, there's wine snobbery, a fairy-tale feast and a Moroccan menu based solely on one couple's love of hummus!
...Selling: Lori's a self-proclaimed hoarder, making her and Michael's property tough to sell. Jonathan starts a cutthroat clean up as Drew clears the path to find a perfect place.
The Caribbean Carnival One: Laid-back Daniel fancies a Caribbean street parade for his nuptials, while Sabrina dreams of a more traditional event. Will their big day be carnage or carnival?
The Beauty and the Beast One: Beauty queen and bride-to-be Yasmin has her heart set on a fairytale wedding. Can powerlifter Yiannis deliver with his Beauty and the Beast-themed day?
Lorne Abony, the CEO and chairman of in-store media provider Mood Media, one of the fastest-growing companies in America, adopts a disguise to go undercover in his own business.
Jim Rogers, the chairman and CEO of Kampgrounds of America, the largest campsite organisation in the world, goes undercover in his business, disguised as an accountant.
Bella Luna: Gordon visits Italian family restaurant Bella Luna, which is struggling with an inexperienced chef and an owner who won't acknowledge the eatery's flaws.
Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he's helped: La Galleria 33 in Boston; The Olde Hitching Post in Massachusetts; and the Prohibition Grille in Washington.
...and Selling: Brand new series: The brothers take charge when single mum Lolita's home needs overhauling. Navigating a red-hot real estate market, can she take the heat?
Ibiza, Baby!: Hit reality spin-off. Captain Glenn and the Parsifal III team return for another sizzling season cruising the party capital of the world: Ibiza!
Greg Davies' assistant Alex Horne is powerless in the face of Ed Gamble's creative vision. Plus: bare chests and bucking donkeys.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jason Manford and Joe Wilkinson take on Lee Mack and Fay Ripley. Alex Horne and The Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
...Christmas Special: Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Catherine Tate take on Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson. Joe Lycett joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
The Funeral: It's Uncle Saul's funeral, which means that 'Horrible Grandma' is back, and leaving a trail of broken furniture in her wake.
For Sale: The boys arrive home to find a 'For Sale' sign in front of the house. How will they stop Mum and Dad from selling the family home?
The team help the survivor of a horrific house fire who wants to cover his scarring with a Celtic knot design, and a woman who wears make-up to cover her shocking neck tattoo.
Greg Davies' assistant Alex Horne is powerless in the face of Ed Gamble's creative vision. Plus: bare chests and bucking donkeys.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jason Manford and Joe Wilkinson take on Lee Mack and Fay Ripley. Alex Horne and The Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Warren Boone, the chief people officer of restaurant chain Coco's Bakery, steps off the corporate ladder to get a taste of life on the company's front line.
Mayor of Fontana, Acquanetta Warren, goes undercover in the Californian city to spend time with sewer workers and the fire brigade to see how the city is recovering from Covid.
In Bournemouth, there are hard-to-please guests, intriguing photos and interesting impressions, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
In South Wales, Rachael and Nick cook dishes they've never made before, Ameel shows off his powers of memory recall, and Angela bosses Jeff around as they prepare their no-nonsense menu.
...Selling: Nyahsa and Hezekiyah's hopes of fixing up their home quickly turned into a nightmare. Can Jonathan and Drew dream up an innovative solution?
The One with All the Pranks: Perfectionist Maria places her dream of a country house wedding in the hands of her fiancé Sam. But Sam loves a good prank. Could this be a problem?
The Drive-in One: Mummy's boy Tiago plans to put his self-conscious bride Karlette on the big screen at his 1950s drive-in wedding, but she wants something intimate and contemporary.
Paul Damico, president of Moe's Southwest Grill, one of the world's fastest-growing restaurant chains, goes undercover to find out how efficient his organisation is.
Sara Bittorf, the chief brand officer of Boston Market - whose 470 restaurants serve home-style comfort food - goes undercover to work alongside the chain's employees.
Roosevelt Inn: Despite being over a million dollars in debt, the owner of the Roosevelt Inn in Coeur d'Alene, Idaho is busy cracking jokes and playing dress up. Can Gordon help?
Cambridge Hotel: Gordon visits Cambridge, NY, where ex-military man John Imhof runs the Cambridge Hotel like a dictatorship, alienating his staff and guests.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Andrea and Santino's dream of moving back downtown is a waking nightmare until the brothers step in with massive renovations.
Tender Bender: Gary crashes the tender. Danni and Keith continue to flirt, but a cute host catches Danni's eye. And Gary and Daisy's friendship is still rocky.
Our five comics devise new ways to help Alex Horne make the Taskmaster's breakfast. Plus: former series champion Kerry Godliman makes a dramatic return.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson take on Kevin Bridges and Jessica Knappett. Dr John Cooper Clarke's in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts, as Alan Carr and Josh Widdicombe take on Joe Wilkinson and Sara Pascoe. Bill Bailey is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent. With Rachel Riley.
The Other Jackie: The award-winning comedy about the Goodman family. Mum and Dad get a hot tub, and Jim brings a date round for a disastrous dinner.
The Tin of Meat: Mum's friend Val moves in and causes havoc when she finds out that Dad is hiding a tin of 20-year-old meat in his shed.
